tests.output_sample:
Browse and call any API endpoint using the raw interface names.

The friendly top-level commands cover the most common operations.
This command provides access to ALL endpoints for power users and
agents that need full API coverage.

Run 'api' with no arguments to list all interfaces.
Run 'api <interface>' to see that interface's methods.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli api [interface] [flags]

Examples:
  # List all available interfaces
  everbee-pp-cli api

  # Show methods for a specific interface
  everbee-pp-cli api <interface-name>

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for api

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'everbee-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

tests.reason: command path [api] has fewer segments than placeholders (1)
command path [api] has fewer segments than placeholders (1)

Export paginated API data to a local file. Supports JSONL (one JSON object
per line, streaming-friendly) and JSON (array). JSONL is recommended for
large datasets as it has no memory pressure.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> [id] [flags]

Examples:
  # Export all items as JSONL (streaming, recommended for large datasets)
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> --format jsonl --output data.jsonl

  # Export with limit
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> --format jsonl --limit 1000

  # Pipe to another tool
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> --format jsonl | jq '.id'

Flags:
      --format string   Output format: jsonl or json (default "jsonl")
  -h, --help            help for export
      --limit int       Maximum records to export (0 = unlimited)
      --no-cache        Bypass response cache for fresh data
  -o, --output string   Output file path (default: stdout)

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'everbee-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

Import data from a JSONL file by issuing POST requests for each record.
Each line must be a valid JSON object. Failed records are logged to stderr
but do not stop the import.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli import <resource> [flags]

Examples:
  # Import from a JSONL file
  everbee-pp-cli import <resource> --input data.jsonl

  # Dry-run to preview without sending
  everbee-pp-cli import <resource> --input data.jsonl --dry-run

  # Import from stdin
  cat data.jsonl | everbee-pp-cli import <resource> --input -

Flags:
      --batch-size int   Records per batch (future: batch API support) (default 1)
      --dry-run          Preview import without sending requests
  -h, --help             help for import
  -i, --input string     Input JSONL file path (use - for stdin)

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'everbee-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

Search data using FTS5 full-text search on locally synced data,
or hit the API's search endpoint when available.

In auto mode (default): uses the API search endpoint if the API has one,
otherwise searches local data. Falls back to local on network failure.
In live mode: uses the API search endpoint only.
In local mode: searches locally synced data only.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli search <query> [flags]

Examples:
  # Search (uses API endpoint if available, local FTS otherwise)
  everbee-pp-cli search "error timeout"

  # Force local search only
  everbee-pp-cli search "payment failed" --data-source local

  # Search a specific resource type locally
  everbee-pp-cli search "critical" --type transactions --data-source local

  # JSON output for piping
  everbee-pp-cli search "critical" --json --limit 20

Flags:
      --db string     Database path (default: ~/.local/share/everbee-pp-cli/data.db)

Sync data from the API into a local SQLite database. Supports resumable
incremental sync (only fetches new data since last sync) and full resync.
Once synced, use the 'search' command for instant full-text search.

Exit codes & warnings:
  Resources the API denies access to (HTTP 403, or HTTP 400 with an
  access-policy body) are reported as warnings rather than failing the
  run. In --json mode each is emitted as a {"event":"sync_warning",...}
  line carrying status, reason, and message fields, and a final
  {"event":"sync_summary",...} aggregates the run.

  Exit 0 when at least one resource synced and no resource flagged in
  the spec as critical (x-critical: true) failed; non-critical failures
  emit {"event":"sync_warning","reason":"exit_policy_default_changed",
  ...} so callers can detect that a partial failure was tolerated. Pass
  --strict to exit non-zero on any per-resource failure. Exit is always
  non-zero when every selected resource failed, regardless of --strict.

Resource scoping:
  --resources runs the named top-level resources, plus any parent-keyed
  dependent whose own name is listed OR whose parent table is listed.
  Naming a parent therefore cascades to its dependents (so "sync this
  parent and its children" works without listing every nested resource
  by hand). There is no flag today to suppress the cascade for a named
  parent. To run a dependent without re-syncing its parent, list only
  the dependent by name; the parent table must already be populated
  from a prior sync.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli sync [flags]

Examples:
  # Sync all resources
  everbee-pp-cli sync

  # Sync specific resources only
  everbee-pp-cli sync --resources channels,messages

  # Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
  everbee-pp-cli sync --full

  # Incremental sync: only records from the last 7 days
  everbee-pp-cli sync --since 7d

  # Parallel sync with 8 workers
  everbee-pp-cli sync --concurrency 8

  # Latest-only: refresh head of each resource, no historical backfill
  everbee-pp-cli sync --latest-only

Flags:
      --concurrency int              Number of parallel sync workers (default 4)
      --db string                    Database path (default: ~/.local/share/everbee-pp-cli/data.db)
      --full                         Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
      --global-param stringArray     Extra query param to inject into every sync request including dependent path-scoped calls (repeatable, key=value). Use when an API requires a scope on every call regardless of path nesting.
  -h, --help                         help for sync
      --latest-only                  Refresh head of each resource only; clears resume cursor and caps pages at 1. Mutually exclusive with --since (--since wins).
      --max-pages int                Maximum pages to fetch per resource (0 = unlimited; cap-hit emits a sync_warning event) (default 100)
      --param stringArray            Extra query param to inject into flat-list sync requests (repeatable, key=value). Skipped on path-scoped dependent requests so a top-level scope like workspace=<id> does not double up on /parents/<id>/children calls. Use --global-param to inject everywhere. Avoid pagination keys (limit/since/cursor) — overriding them corrupts resume state.
      --resource-param stringArray   Per-resource extra query param (repeatable, resource:key=value). Wins over --param and --global-param when keys conflict.
      --resources strings            Comma-separated resource types to sync. Naming a parent also runs its parent-keyed dependents (see Long help for scoping).
      --since string                 Incremental sync duration (e.g. 7d, 24h, 1w, 30m)
      --strict                       Exit non-zero on any per-resource failure (default: only critical failures or all-resource failure exit non-zero).

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        …[truncated]

which resolves a natural-language capability query (for example, "search messages" or "stale tickets") to the best matching command from this CLI's curated feature index.

Exit codes:
  0  at least one match found
  2  no confident match - the query did not score against any indexed capability; fall back to '--help' or 'search' if this CLI has one

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli which [query] [flags]

Examples:
  everbee-pp-cli which "stale tickets"
  everbee-pp-cli which "bottleneck"
  everbee-pp-cli which --limit 1 "send message"
  everbee-pp-cli which                                # list the full capability index

Flags:
  -h, --help        help for which
      --limit int   Maximum number of matches to return (default 3)

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'everbee-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

tests.reason: command path [which] has fewer segments than placeholders (1)

tests.output_sample:
Archive fetches all syncable resources from the API and stores them in a
local SQLite database. Supports incremental sync (only new data since last run)
and full resync. After archiving, use 'search' for instant full-text search.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli workflow archive [flags]

Examples:
  # Archive all resources
  everbee-pp-cli workflow archive

  # Full re-archive (ignore previous sync state)
  everbee-pp-cli workflow archive --full

Flags:
      --db string   Database path (default: ~/.local/share/everbee-pp-cli/data.db)
      --full        Full re-archive (ignore previous sync state)
  -h, --help        help for archive

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'everbee-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

Show local archive status and sync state for all resources

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli workflow status [flags]

Examples:
  # Show archive status
  everbee-pp-cli workflow status

  # Show status as JSON
  everbee-pp-cli workflow status --json

Flags:
      --db string   Database path
  -h, --help        help for status

Global Flags:
